text: Porous heterogeneous catalysts represent one of the most significant components in modern industrial chemistry. Herein, we demonstrate an effective way to synthesize a new hafnium-based metal-organic framework, Hf-NU-1000 with Hf6 clusters. As a proof of concept, the catalytic performance toward activation of epoxides and subsequent utilization in CO2-to-cyclic carbonates conversion as well as various other transformations were explored. Notably, quantitative CO2 fixation into five-membered cyclic carbonates is easily achieved within one atmosphere by employing only a small amount of catalyst. Meanwhile, Hf-NU-1000 could also be applied for the regioselective and enantioselective synthesis of 1,2-bifunctional products by means of solvolytic nucleophilic ring-opening of corresponding strained cyclic molecules, demonstrating its significant application in the precision organic transformation.
